observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the following:
Municipal Accountant to record transactions that are cash advances in nature with the appropriate Advances account(s) applicable to the disbursing officer and not as outright expenses; and
Acting Municipal Treasurer to prepare Report of Disbursements (ROD) for the succeeding cash advances.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:
Require the Municipal Treasurer to submit a report providing details on the collections and uncollected rentals from the lessee to the Municipal Accountant to serve as a basis in the recording of transactions and collect the unpaid stall rentals from the lessee;
Direct the Municipal Accountant to recognize the total unpaid rentals in the books of the Municipality to the appropriate receivable and income account based on the Certified List of Stallholders with the duly approved Contract of Lease and provide subsidiary ledgers for each stallholder; and
Strictly enforce lease agreements/rights and the ordinances by requiring all lessees to sign updated lease agreements with clear terms on rental payments, penalties for late payments, and consequences of non-compliance; and
Appoint or hire a Market Supervisor or Municipal Economic Enterprise and Development Officer (MEEDO) to oversee and manage all economic enterprises of the Municipality effectively.

We recommended the management to require the Municipal Accountant to reconcile the balances of the reciprocal accounts under GF, SEF and TF and make the necessary corrections/adjustments. Henceforth, to closely monitor the transactions affecting the reciprocal accounts and ensure that these are recorded in the different funds simultaneously to avoid errors and omissions.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:
Ensure that at the end of each year, the Municipal Accountant transfers all the unexpended/unobligated balance of the QRF and the DRRMF - MOOE to the Special Trust Fund under the account “Trust Liability - DRRM”;
Require the Municipal Accountant to transfer the unexpended QRF of CY 2024 to the Special Trust Fund under the account Trust Liabilities-DRRM; and
Conduct a thorough reconciliation of the Trust Liability - DRRMF account with the Notes to Financial Statements and Utilization Reports to resolve the discrepancies amounting to ₱97,774.17 and ₱(879,921.09) and ensure the accuracy and reliability of financial records.
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive to require the Municipal Treasurer to release the CY 2024 and prior year’s balance of RPT Share and financial aid to the barangays immediately.

We recommended to the management to:
Direct the GSO to cause the immediate marking of all the vehicles and strictly observe all the rules and regulations on the use of government vehicles;
Secure the government plates for the recently acquired vehicles to the supplier/seller to expedite the processing of the permanent red plates issued by the Land Transportation Office (LTO); and
Require the GSO to ensure that all motor vehicles, heavy equipment, and machinery of the LGU have insurance coverage with GSIS and to pay the annual insurance regularly.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive to:
Direct the Municipal Budget Officer to ensure that the appropriation for EME in the Annual Budget complies with the provisions prescribed in Section 325 (h) of RA No. 7160 and that the actual expenses sourced therefrom are within the limitation; and
Direct the Municipal Accountant observes the proper use of accounts provided in the COA Chart of Accounts in recording financial transactions.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive to instruct the Municipal Accountant to enforce strict compliance with procurement and disbursement documentation requirements, implement a thorough pre-audit process, and require the submission of complete supporting documents before processing payments to prevent irregular transactions.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:
Direct the Municipal Engineer to prepare the Statement of Work Accomplished (SWA) every month for every infrastructure project implemented by administration; and
Direct the Municipal Accountant to ensure that payments of labor for every project shall be supported with Program of Work, Inspection Report by the Municipal Engineer, Pictures of items of work and Certificate of Completion.

We recommended to the Local Chief Executive the following:
Direct the Municipal Accountant to ensure that contributions are remitted on time and in full within the prescribed period to prevent further financial penalties;
Require HRMO to regularly verify employee contribution records to prevent issues when availing of benefits; and
Direct the Municipal Treasurer to immediately settle the past due balances with the agency.

We recommended to the Local School Board to:
Refrain from charging non-priority expenditure against Special Education Fund;
Evaluate the programs/projects/activities to be included in the SEF budget, taking into consideration the objectives set forth by the provisions of DepEd-DBM-DILG Joint Circular No. 1, s. 2017; and
Submit the related disbursement vouchers and provide a thorough justification for the expenditures that have been identified as non-compliant.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:
Require the LSB to formulate/prepare the required Three-Year Strategic Program and consider priorities indicated in the School Investment Plan (SIP) and the Division Education Development Plan (DEDP) to serve as a reference in the preparation of the SEF budget;
Instruct the Municipal Budget Office to submit the Dep-Ed SIP and DEDP together with the Annual Budget in the ensuing years; and
Direct the LSB to incorporate ECCD programs, projects and activities in the SEF Annual Budget.

We recommended the Local Chief Executive to direct the Municipal Treasurer immediately transfer the remaining balance to the designated Development Fund account and ensure that future NTA allocations are fully and promptly transferred upon receipt. Additionally, regular reconciliation of fund balances and project monitoring should be enforced to ensure that all 20% DF transactions are properly accounted for and utilized strictly for development projects.

We recommended to the Local Chief Executive to direct the Acting Municipal Treasurer to comply with COA Circular No. 2012-002 by ensuring that at least five percent (5%) of the estimated revenue from regular sources is fully transferred to the LDRRMF.

We recommended to the Local Chief Executive to direct the MDRRMO to refrain from using the DRRMF for programs/projects/activities (PPAs) which are not directly related with pre-disaster preparedness, mitigation, response and rescue operations to ensure that it is utilized solely for approved DRRM related PPAs.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended to the Local Chief Executive to:
Appoint or designate a General Service Officer who shall specifically take custody of all the properties of the LGU and prepare and maintain stock cards and property cards for it; create an inventory committee that shall perform a physical inventory of all properties of the Municipality and submit to the Audit Team the Report on Physical count of PPE (RCPPE) and Report of the Physical Count of Inventories (RPCI);
Instruct the Municipal Accountant to maintain and update Inventories and PPE Ledger Cards for each category of inventory and PPE and to conduct periodic reconciliation of balances thereof with that of the GSO records; and
Require the Municipal Accountant to compute the depreciation on the equipment accounts and make the necessary adjustment on the affected accounts.

We recommended to the Local Chief Executive to:
Direct the Municipal Accountant to record transactions that are cash advances in nature with the appropriate Advances account(s) applicable and not as outright expenses. Make the necessary adjusting entries;
Require the Municipal Accountant to submit DVs and supporting documents for unsubmitted accounts of the Municipal Treasurer summarized in Annex E, page 119-123; and
Direct the Acting Municipal Treasurer to prepare Report of Disbursements (ROD) for the succeeding cash advances.

We recommended to the Municipal Accountant to:
Record the transactions in accordance with the prescribed Philippine Government Chart of Accounts under the NGAS Manual, COA Circular 2004-008 dated September 20, 2004. Fund transfers should be debited to Due from LGUs and will only be credited upon submission of liquidation documents by recipient agencies; and
Require that all fund transfers to LGUs/Barangays should be supported by a Memorandum of Agreement, Approved Program of Work and other necessary documents as provided in Section 3.1.1 of COA Circular 2012-001. The MOA should explicitly provide the mechanics of the release, the accounting, and reporting of the fund.

We recommended to the Local Chief Executive to direct all Collecting Officer to remit their collection on a daily basis, or in the case the collections are minimal and daily deposit becomes impractical, to turn-over the collections at least once a week or as soon as their collections reach ₱10,000.00.

We recommended to the Municipal accountant to:
Ensure that the purpose of cash advances are on the face of the disbursement vouchers and that they are duly supported with complete documents at the time of grant and ensure that cash advances for payroll fund shall be equal to the net amount of the payroll for the pay period;
Discontinue the practice of disbursing funds through cash advances to pay for the Municipality’s operating expenses but instead pay directly under the name of the payees, concerned employees and suppliers, ensuring that the transactions of the LGU are always supported with complete documentation that would ascertain their propriety and validity; and
Refrain from granting multiple cash advances to the Acting Municipal Treasurer and ensure that his previous cash advance has been liquidated before granting additional cash advance.

We recommended the Local Chief Executive to establish specific guidelines setting the rules and regulations on the grants of financial assistance and adequate measures in implementing and granting financial assistance to individuals/recipients.

We recommended to the Local Chief Executive to require the concerned officials and employees to:
Prepare the Report of Checks Issued (RCI) with checks presented chronologically and numerically, ensuring that all checks released or unreleased to claimants are included, along with the cancelled/voided checks;
Strictly comply with the requirements under COA Circular No. 92-389 and Section 32, Chapter 2, Volume II of NGAS Manual to maintain sound internal control in the disbursement of government funds through properly accomplishing the Disbursement Vouchers, particularly on the numbering and proper listing of the supporting documents on the face of the DV and the perforation of the paid Disbursement Vouchers and payrolls, including all supporting documents, to avoid their reuse; and
Properly and completely accomplish relevant procurement documents such as PR, RFQ, Abstract of Quotation and PO and provide the necessary and relevant specifications of the items to be procured in unambiguous terms for all future procurement projects to serve as reference during inspection and acceptance of deliveries by management.

We recommended to the Local Chief Executive to:
Facilitate immediate submission of the unsubmitted accounts to avoid audit suspension thereof;
Require the Municipal Accountant to submit to the Office of the Auditor the paid DVs and payrolls on or before 10th day of the ensuing month; and
Require the Municipal Treasurer to submit to the Accounting Office all the DVs, ORs including the corresponding reports of each month, on or before the fifth (5) day of the ensuing month.

We recommended to the Local Chief Executive to:
Direct the BAC to submit the required supporting documents to substantiate their claims on honoraria;
Discontinue the inclusion of the Municipal Treasurer as  member of the Technical Working Group (TWG) pursuant to DOF Department Order No. 042-2019, otherwise, subsequent payments will be disallowed in audit; and
Observe strict compliance with implementing rules and regulations of procurement law on the qualifications of Bids and Awards Committee (BAC), BAC Secretariat and Technical Working Group (TWG) and the prescribed rates per DBM Budget Circular No. 2007-5A.

We recommended the management to meticulously and judiciously plan the procurement process of the agency by consolidating the quarterly requirements for similar procurement items based on the PPMP to be incorporated in the APP.

We recommended to the Local Chief Executive to:
Require the Bids and Awards Committee to strictly implement the use of any of the alternative mode of procurement provided under the Revised Implementing Rules and Regulations of R.A. 9184, if public bidding is not feasible; and
Direct the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to ensure that all payments are made by check payable directly to the creditor to whom the money is due.

Direct the Local Development Council to judiciously plan the programs/projects/ activities to be funded out of the 20% Development Fund (DF) in accordance with the mandate of DILD-DBM JMC No. 2017-01 dated February 22, 2017; and

Promptly implement all the programs/projects/activities as embodied in the approved Annual Investment Plan in order to optimize the delivery of basic services to the people. 

Require the MPDC to prepare and submit to the audit team the accomplishment report for the 20% development fund.
observations.reason: There is a revision of programs and constraint due to the pandemic.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended to the Local Chief Executive to:

Direct the head of Human Resource Management Office to submit the contract of service of the job orders with specific duties and functions and reconcile the manpower complement based on the actual manpower and instruct to use the computer-generated DTRs based on the captured biometric date as basis for their preparation of payrolls for the establishment of a reliable number of days worked as the basis for the computation of their wages.

Comply with the aforementioned rules and regulations in the hiring of Job Orders, prepare and issue policies and guidelines to address the issue on the necessity and propriety of hiring JOs.

Immediately strengthen the controls in the hiring of job order by requiring them to submit personal records, undergo interview and document the evaluation of performance to give assurance that the risk identified above are addressed.

Consider either one of the following:

Appoint Job Order personnel as regular employees if they are qualified and believe that their services are essential and contributory to the improvement of agency’s operations or,

Stop hiring Job Order and fill up the vacant plantilla positions in accordance with the Civil Service Commission rules and regulations. Prioritize the undermanned division in the filling-up of vacant plantilla position.

Formulate a policy and internal control in the hiring of job order workers such as but not limited to requiring specific and detailed description of duties and   responsibilities to eliminate task having no definite purpose, if any.

Require the Local Chief Executive and Municipal Budget Officer to explain in writing the excessive expenditure compared to its budget, and to submit supplemental budget, if any. Otherwise, it shall be disallowed in audit.

Disallow the JOs to perform the dutires of AOs as they are not bonded.

Secure certification/justification that such activites/services cannot be provided by regular employees.
observations.managementAction:
The Human Resource Management Office does not submit the contract of service of the job orders and consultants as of December 31, 2019. Moreover, there is an increase number of Job Orders and no reconciliation as to the manpower complement based on the actual manpower. 
The Management does not formulate a policy and internal control in the hiring of job order workers such as but not limited to requiring specific and detailed description of duties and   responsibilities to eliminate workings having no definite purpose, if any.
observations.reason: The Management will evaluate and scrutinize the hiring of job order employees.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended to the Local Chief Executive to:

Require the proponents headed by Municipal Planning and Development with the Engineering department to conduct thorough planning in coming up with timely implementation of proposed projects.

Instruct the heads of the implementing offices to prioritize and expedite the implementation of these projects to provide the expected benefits to the intended beneficiaries on time and maximum use of the programs/projects, and to act promptly on issues or constraints causing the non-implementation of the projects.

Direct the Head of the Agency to submit justification as to the cause of non-implementation of the above-mentioned projects.

Require the Municipal Accountant to return the unused amount of fund transfer or dormant accounts to the source agency.
observations.managementAction: The Management explained that some of the projects were implemented and still on-going. Also, unused balance of some projects were already returned to the respective agencies concern.

observations.recommendation:
Recommendations:
We recommended to the Local Chief Executive the following:
Direct the OIC-Municipal Treasurer for the immediate full restitution of the missing funds.
Direct the persons who made an IOUs/Vales to the OIC-Municipal Treasurer to refund the amount of P2,042,309.
Institute appropriate administrative and/or criminal charges against the Accountable Officer for malversation of public funds pursuant to Article 217 of the Revised Penal Code.
Require Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant to reconcile their balances recorded in their books of accounts at least quarterly, and to submit reconciliation statement to the Audit Team for verification.
Require the Municipal Treasurer to install internal control measures to ensure that all collections be remitted/deposited intact and daily.

Recommendations:
We recommended to the Local Chief Executive the following:
Direct the Collecting Officer for the immediate full restitution of the missing funds.
Direct the persons who made an IOUs/Vales to the Collecting Officer to refund the amount of P624,459. And require her to explain in writing why she allowed IOUs/Vales out of her collections.
Institute appropriate administrative and/or criminal charges against the Accountable Officer for malversation of public funds pursuant to Article 217 of the Revised Penal Code.

Recommendations:
We recommended to the Local Chief Executive to:
Direct the OIC-Municipal Treasurer to explain in writing why such  accountable forms issued were not properly monitored and accounted for.
Require the persons who made an IOUs/Vales to refund the amount of P390,063.
Require the OIC-Municipal Treasurer to determine their average monthly usage of Accountable Forms and on the basis thereof request Accountable Forms in sufficient quantities not to exceed three months' use.
Instruct the OIC-Municipal Treasurer to issue the Accountable Forms in strict numerical sequence with approved Requisition Issue Voucher.
Cause the Revenue Collecting Clerk III to produce and account for the missing Accountable Forms.
Maintain a separate logbook for Accountable Forms and provide all the necessary details like name of accountable officer to whom the accountable forms were issued, signature of accountable officer with date received and date reported totally used, sold or issued in strict observance of Section 95, Volume I of GAAM.
Prepare and submit religiously the Monthly Report of Accountable Forms in accordance with section 37 of COA Circular No. 92-382.

Recommendations:
We have recommended to the Local Chief Executive to:
a.) Institute appropriate administrative and/or criminal charges against the OIC-Municipal Treasurer and the Collecting Officer for malversation of public funds pursuant to Article 217 of the Revised Penal Code.
Require the OIC-Municipal Treasurer as Head of the Treasury Office to explain in writing why issued accountable forms were kept for a long period of time and was not demanded for remittance or deposits by the collecting officer to the Authorized Depository Bank or her office as Municipal Treasurer.
Direct the OIC-Municipal Treasurer to explain in writing why collecting officers were allowed to accept IOUs, vales or other forms of promissory notes out of the cash collections of the Municipality.
Demand from the Officers and Employees to refund the amount of IOUs/vales in the amount of P2,666,768 and if possible require the Municipal Budget Officer to appropriate and obligate the expenditures paid out from the collections.
Refrain from using the collections for outright payment of disbursement.

Recommendations:
We recommended to the Local Chief Executive:
a.) 	Direct the OIC-Municipal Treasurer to require the Collecting Officers delegated with the task of collecting taxes and other revenues of the LGU to apply with the Bureau of Treasury a bond sufficient to cover their cash accountability and monitor the compliance with the bonding requirement among collectors or AOs.
b.)	Strictly prohibit to designate Collecting Officers and employees with Job Order Status to collect and  who were not bonded.
c.)	Review the plantilla position of Treasury Office and deploy personnel/staff with competence, computer literacy, and who possess high-degree of integrity and other core values, where trust and confidence can be reposed upon.
d.)	Require the Municipal Budget Officer to provide budget for bond premium.

Recommendations:
We recommended the Local Chief Executive to:
Direct the OIC-Municipal Treasurer to liquidate immediately her unliquidated cash advances for payroll and operating expenses in the amount of P454,992.
Ensure that the Accountable Officers who were granted cash advance must liquidate their cash advances within the required period provided in item 5.1 of COA Circular No. 97-002 dated February 10, 1997.
Withheld the salary or any money payable to the OIC-Municipal Treasurer until the cash advances has been fully liquidated and settled pursuant to item 5.1 and 9.3.2 of COA Circular No. 97-002 dated February 10, 1997.
Recording of Liquidation should be made on a per cash advance basis and not on a monthly basis.
Record the ATM Payroll in a separate Subsidiary Ledger.
Maintain separate cashbooks for payroll and operating expenses. The Accountable Officer shall record the transactions in the prescribed cashbook daily in accordance with Item 6 of COA Circular No. 97-002 dated February 10, 1997.
Direct the Municipal Treasurer to disburse payment of salaries to job order with approved supporting documents like approved payroll.

Recommendations:
We recommended to the Local Chief Executive to:
Direct the Municipal Accountant to prioritize the preparation and submission of the monthly Bank Reconciliation Statements to achieve an accurate financial reporting of Cash in Bank account and reconcile with the Bank Reconciliation with the general ledgers. Also require him to get the monthly bank statements from the depository bank promptly and regularly;
Require the Acting Municipal Treasurer to reconcile his cashbooks entries with that from the accounting records at least quarterly; and
Give authority to the Municipal Accountant to enroll in LBP i-access so that bank balances can be viewed and printed anytime with the Municipal Treasurer.
Request the Municipal Mayor to hire regular employees as additional staff, if not, fill up the vacant position in the Accounting Office.

Recommendations:
We recommended to the Local Chief Executive to:
Direct Local Chief Executive to create an Inventory Committee to conduct complete physical inventory count of all PPE and submit Report on the Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment (RPCPPE). If possible designate another permanent personnel to handle the General Services Office to cope with the requirements.
Instruct the General Services Officer/Supply Officer-Designate and the Municipal Accountant to regularly reconcile their records and review the composition of the PPE account to come up with an accurate Property, Plant and Equipment account balance.
Require the General Services Officer/Supply Officer-Designate to issue property tags/ stickers for each PPE in order to identify and trace the location of the said PPEs and to issue corresponding Acknowledgement Receipt for Equipment (ARE) for all the Properties, Plant and Equipment issued to officials and employees who have the custody thereto and to facilitate such undertaking, assign additional competent personnel in order to complete the needed property records.
Require the Accouting Office and Supply Officer-Designate to maintain an updated PPE Ledger Cards and Property Cards and must conduct reconciliation of their reports. Coordination with the Municipal Assessor for the valuation of some properties with no cost or those acquired in prior years.

Recommendations:
We recommended to the Local Chief Executive to:
Direct the Municipal Engineer to provide the Municipal Accountant, General Services Officer with the complete description and cost segregation of road components for road projects;
Direct the General Services Officer-Designate to maintain the Local Road Inventory and Road Map, and to keep a complete Local Road Network Property Card for all roads and its components; and
Require the Municipal Accountant the following reporting procedures:
Prepare the Journal Voucher (JV) to record the beginning balance of the local road network and its components in the general ledger and the Local Road Network Ledger Card, respectively. Support the JV with the Registry of Public Infrastructures for Roads, working paper on the distribution of costs for the road components, and working paper on the determination of the depreciated replacement cost for road components with no available cost per registry.
Keep and maintain subsidiary records for roads and road components for every road network; and
Prepare a lapsing schedule for the computation of the depreciation for each component at the end of the year.

Recommendations:
We have recommended to the Local Chief Executive to:
a.) Direct the Municipal Treasurer to submit disbursement vouchers after payment with complete supporting documents to the Office of Municipal Accountant.
b.) Direct the Municipal Accountant to strictly adhere to the prescribed time frame for the submission of monthly and quarterly reports as well as transmitting all receipts and disbursement records with complete supporting documents to the Office of the Auditor.
c.) Direct the Municipal Accountant to closely coordinate with the Municipal Treasurer for the latter to submit immediately the required reports within the prescribed period, and to facilitate the recording of the transactions in the books of accounts.
d.) Request the Local Chief Executive and the Municipal Treasurer to enroll in LBP i-access so that bank balances can be viewed and printed anytime.
e.) 	Request the Municipal Mayor to hire regular employees as additional staff, if not, fill up the vacant position in the Accounting Office.

Recommendations:
We recommended to the Local Chief Executive to:
Direct the HRMO to meticulously review all the employees DTRs before forwarding them to the Accounting Office for payroll preparation. Likewise, require the Department Head to check and monitor the attendance of their staff.
Direct the HRMO to compute the undertimes of the employees at the start of their service and deduct them from their leave credit balances.
Require the employees to submit approved application of leave and deduct it also from their vacation leave or sick leave balances.
Impose administrative sanctions as embodied in CSC rules and regulations.
Secure that applications of leave of the employees are approved.
Direct the HRMO/Municipal Accounant to not include employees in the payroll who have no DTRs.

Recommendations:
We recommended to the Local Chief Executive to:
Direct the Department Heads to thoroughly check and verify the DTRs of the job order employees against the logbook, before forwarding them to the Office of the HRMO.
Direct the HRMO to check and compute the undertimes and absences of the job order employees and the same shall be deducted from their salary/payroll.
Management should impose administrative sanctions to erring job order employees, and establish strong internal control in the payroll system.
Submit summary report of undertimes/absences to the Audit Team and require the job orders to immediately refund overpayment.
Require the job order employees to submit the lacking documents and refund overpayment of wages as indicated under Appendix FF and GG.

Recommendation:
We recommended to the Local Chief Executive to submit approved Alternative Work Arrangement (AWA) and direct the concerned personnel to adopt and strictly abide with the provisions of the existing rules and regulations governing the alternative work arrangements as basis for monitoring their daily work schedule and accomplishment. Likewise, submit a report on the said implementation for policy formulation and records purposes.

Recommendations:
We recommended to the Local Chief Executive to:
Direct the head of Human Resource Management Office to submit the contract of service of the job orders with specific duties and functions and reconcile the manpower complement based on the actual manpower and instruct to use the computer-generated DTRs based on the captured biometric date as basis for their preparation of payrolls for the establishment of a reliable number of days worked as the basis for the computation of their wages.
Comply with the aforementioned rules and regulations in the hiring of Job Orders, prepare and issue policies and guidelines to address the issue on the necessity and propriety of hiring JOs.
Immediately strengthen the controls in the hiring of job order by requiring them to submit personal records, undergo interview and document the evaluation of performance to give assurance that the risk identified above are addressed.
Consider either one of the following:
Appoint Job Order personnel as regular employees if they are qualified and believe that their services are essential and contributory to the improvement of agency’s operations or,
Stop hiring Job Order and fill up the vacant plantilla positions in accordance with the Civil Service Commission rules and regulations. Prioritize the undermanned division in the filling-up of vacant plantilla position.
Formulate a policy and internal control in the hiring of job order workers such as but not limited to requiring specific and detailed description of duties and   responsibilities to eliminate task having no definite purpose, if any.
Require the Local Chief Executive and Municipal Budget Officer to explain in writing the excessive expenditure compared to its budget, and to submit supplemental budget, if any. Otherwise, it shall be disallowed in audit.
Disallow the JOs to perform the dutires of AOs as they are not bonded.
Secure certification/justification that such activites/services cannot be provided by regular employees.

Recommendations:
We recommended to the Local Chief Executive to:
Require the proponents headed by Municipal Planning and Development with the Engineering department to conduct thorough planning in coming up with timely implementation of proposed projects.
Instruct the heads of the implementing offices to prioritize and expedite the implementation of these projects to provide the expected benefits to the intended beneficiaries on time and maximum use of the programs/projects, and to act promptly on issues or constraints causing the non-implementation of the projects.
Direct the Head of the Agency to submit justification as to the cause of non-implementation of the above-mentioned projects.
Require the Municipal Accountant to return the unused amount of fund transfer or dormant accounts to the source agency.

Recommendations:
We recommended to the Local Chief Executive to:
Direct the Municipal Planning and Development with the Engineering     department to plan in carrying out accurate design and effective production of the projects in order to ensure immediate implementation when the funds are available, and
Instruct the heads of the implementing offices to expedite the implementation of these projects to provide the expected benefits to the intended beneficiaries on time and to act promptly on issues or constraints causing the delay of the implementation of the projects.
Require the Municipal Engineering Office to explain in writing as to the cause of delay in the implementation of the projects.
Require the Local Chief Executive through the Municipal Engineer to file and demand payment for liquidated damages for the number of days delayed. Otherwise, issue a Blacklisting Order disqualifying the erring contractor from participating in the bidding of all government projects.

Recommendation:
We recommended to the Local Chief Executive to:
a.) Direct through the Municipal Engineer to file and demand payment for liquidated damages for the number of days delayed. And issue a Blacklisting Order disqualifying the erring contractor from participating in the bidding of all government projects.
b.) Require the Municipal Engineer to submit Status/Updated Report of the project so that appropriate action could be made by the Management.
c.) Oblige the management to strictly comply the Implementing Rules and Regulations of RA 9184 and keep updated of GPPB issuances.
Submit justification on their failure to rescind the contract on time and in action.

Recommendations:
We recommended to the Local Chief Executive to:
a.) Require the Municipal Accountant to submit the lacking documents to ensure the completeness and propriety of the required supporting documents for all disbursements using the Bayanihan Grant to Municipalities.
b.) Direct the Management to be knowledgeable of the new laws, rules and regulations in the government.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended to the Local Chief Executive to:

a.) Establish a system in the withdrawal of fuel, oil and lubricants from the LGU to supplier such as:

i.) The driver will submit a requisition/request form duly numbered for approval by the General Services Officer and attach the duly filled up Drivers Trip Tickets.

ii.)	Require the Supplier to submit Statement of Account (SOA) duly supported with the Charge Invoices.

b.) The GSO should designate a personnel to prepare the Monthly Report of Official Travels and the Monthly Report of Fuel Consumption for submission to the Municipal Accountant.

c.) 	Compel the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) to conduct public bidding in the procurement of fuel, oil and lubricants. Otherwise, other mode of procurement will be properly recommended by the BAC.

d.) The Municipal Accountant should refrain from processing of claims without the above-mentioned documents.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant required the drivers, accomplished driver’s trip ticket, travel order and other necessary documents.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended to Local Chief Executive to:

Direct the Municipal Accountant to immediately strengthen the controls in the payment of terminal leave benefits to give assurance that the risk identified above is addressed. 

Require the Human Resource Management Officer to prepare a summary of leave credit of all employees at the end of the year for employees’ information and submit to the Office of Municipal Accountant; 

Direct the Municipal Budget Officer, Municipal Accountant and Human Resource Management Officer to explain the cause of the overpayment of terminal leave benefits, and require the payee to make a refund on the excess claim.

Strictly adhere to the Omnibus Rules on Leave and be judicious and frugal in its expenses in consonance with COA Circular 2012-003.
observations.managementAction: The Local Government Unit call the attention of the concerned personnel and explain the cause of the overpayment of the terminal leave.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive to:

Instruct the Bids and Awards Committee to properly plan the procurement of goods and supplies and strictly observe the Implementing Rules and Regulation on RA No. 9184 or the Procurement Law. Competitive Bidding should be the primary mode of procurement in compliance with RA 9184 to promote transparency, economy and efficiency in the procurement process. 

Require the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to draw check payments directly in the name of the supplier in conformity with existing regulations and withhold the appropriate taxes due the government.
observations.managementAction: The BAC properly plan the procurement of goods and supplies and strictly adhere to the Implementing Rules and Regulations of RA 9184. The LGU followed the competitive bidding as the primary mode of procurement in compliance with the said law.
observations.reason: The BAC properly plan the procurement of goods and supplies and strictly adhere to the Implementing Rules and Regulations of RA 9184. The LGU followed the competitive bidding as the primary mode of procurement in compliance with the said law.

observations.recommendation: We recommended to the Local Chief Executive to direct the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) Secretariat to comply with the regulations and furnish the Audit Team copies of the contract agreements together with the corresponding supporting documents within the prescribed period for the timely review, evaluation and inspection of the same.
observations.managementAction: The BAC will comply to the submission of technical documents as required by your office based on the prescribed Bidding procedure and protocol based RA 9184. The BAC Secretariat shall comply with the regulations and furnish the audit team with the required copy of contracts along with their supporting documents within the prescribed period for timely review, evaluation and inspection. We will submit to your office copies of the contracts before issuance of award and notice to proceed. The Municipal Engineer, Supply Officer and the End User of the Procurement with the Accounting Office shall facilitate the request and conduct of technical inspection for all implemented projects/
observations.reason: The BAC- Secretarial will comply submission of contracts within the prescribed period.

observations.recommendation: We recommended to the Local Chief Executive to require the GAD Focal Person to prepare and submit the corresponding GAD Accomplishment Report within the period prescribed under COA Circular No. 2014-001 dated March 18, 2014.
observations.managementAction: The Local Chief Executive required the GAD Focal Person to submit the GAD Accomplishment Report for CY2019
observations.reason: GAD Focal Person submitted the GAD Accomplishment Report to the Office of the Auditor.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive to:

Direct the focal point person to spearhead the establishment of GAD database to serve as basis for gender-responsive planning, programming, and policy formulation which will form part of the overall management information system of the agency. 

Establish a GAD monitoring and evaluation (M&E) system as required under Section 5.1 of Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-01.
observations.managementAction: The Local Chief Executive has designated the MSWDO as GAD Focal Person and  was tasked to prepare corresponding quarterly GAD Accomplishment Report based from the GAD database and its submission and posting to the Full Disclosure Bulletin Portal.
observations.reason: Had difficulty in preparing the GAD Plan and Budget and its Accomplishment Report.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended to the Local Chief Executive to:

Direct the Municipal Planning and Development with the Engineering     department to plan in carrying out accurate design and effective production of the projects in order to ensure immediate implementation when the funds are available, and

Instruct the heads of the implementing offices to expedite the implementation of these projects to provide the expected benefits to the intended beneficiaries on time and to act promptly on issues or constraints causing the delay of the implementation of the projects.

Require the Municipal Engineering Office to explain in writing as to the cause of delay in the implementation of the projects.

Require the Local Chief Executive through the Municipal Engineer to file and demand payment for liquidated damages for the number of days delayed. Otherwise, issue a Blacklisting Order disqualifying the erring contractor from participating in the bidding of all government projects.
observations.managementAction: The Local Chief Executive to has directed the Municipal Planning and Development Office with the Engineering Department to plan in carrying out accurate design and effective production of the projects  in order to ensure immediate implementation when the funds are available.
observations.reason: The Management explained that the delay of the projects due to rewiring and widening projects.

observations.recommendation: We recommended to the Local Chief Executive and to the Members of the Local School Board to closely monitor and strategically plan and implement the approved programs, projects and activities under the Special Education Fund and maximize the utilization of SEF to attain its objectives for the benefit of the constituents.
observations.managementAction: The Local School Board has planned and ensured that the projects for the Special Education Fund (SEF) be optimally utilized intended for the students, schools and teachers.

observations.recommendation:
Direct the OIC-Municipal Treasurer to explain in writing why such  accountable forms issued were not properly monitored and accounted for.
Require the persons who made an IOUs/Vales to refund the amount of P390,063.
Require the OIC-Municipal Treasurer to determine their average monthly usage of Accountable Forms and on the basis thereof request Accountable Forms in sufficient quantities not to exceed three months' use.
Instruct the OIC-Municipal Treasurer to issue the Accountable Forms in strict numerical sequence with approved Requisition Issue Voucher.
Cause the Revenue Collecting Clerk III to produce and account for the missing Accountable Forms
Maintain a separate logbook for Accountable Forms and provide all the necessary details in strict observance of Section 95, Volume I of GAAM.
Prepare and submit religiously the Monthly Report of Accountable Forms in accordance with section 37 of COA Circular No. 92-382.
observations.managementAction:
The Agency required the former OIC-Municipal Treasurer to submit reconciliation of accountable forms with & without money value. 
The new Municipal Treasurer maintained logbook and religiously prepared reconciliation and monthly report of  Accountable forms.
observations.reason: Management exerted effort to comply the audit recommendations.

observations.recommendation:
Require the OIC-Municipal Treasurer and Collecting Officers to diligently and regularly update the posting of her cashbook; record collections and deposits on a daily basis for easy determination, monitoring and verification of balances.
Cause the Collecting Officer to maintain separate cashbook for each fund handled in accordance with what the National Government Accounting System Manual for LGUs prescribes.
Direct both the Municipal Accountant and the OIC-Municipal Treasurer for immediate reconciliation of their records at least quarterly.
Require the OIC- Municipal Treasurer to submit justification on the above-mentioned deficiencies.
observations.managementAction: Instruct the Municipal Treasurer to update collection and cashbook, properly record the transactions to avoid errors and judicially follow the guidelines of COA Circular No. 97-002. Moreover, Municipal Accountant verbally instructed MTO and bookkeeper to reconcile the records.
observations.reason:
The previous OIC-Municipal Treasurer failed to properly maintained a cashbook.

The newly installed Municipal Treasurer kept a new separate cashbook for each bank account, collections and cash advances.

observations.recommendation:
Recommendations:
We recommended to the Local Chief Executive the following:
Direct the OIC-Municipal Treasurer for the immediate full restitution of the missing funds.
Direct the persons who made an IOUs/Vales to the OIC-Municipal Treasurer to refund the amount of P2,042,309.
Institute appropriate administrative and/or criminal charges against the Accountable Officer for malversation of public funds pursuant to Article 217 of the Revised Penal Code.
Require Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant to reconcile their balances recorded in their books of accounts at least quarterly, and to submit reconciliation statement to the Audit Team for verification.
Require the Municipal Treasurer to install internal control measures to ensure that all collections be remitted/deposited intact and daily.

Recommendations:
We recommended to the Local Chief Executive the following:
Direct the Collecting Officer for the immediate full restitution of the missing funds.
Direct the persons who made an IOUs/Vales to the Collecting Officer to refund the amount of P624,459. And require her to explain in writing why she allowed IOUs/Vales out of her collections.
Institute appropriate administrative and/or criminal charges against the Accountable Officer for malversation of public funds pursuant to Article 217 of the Revised Penal Code.

Recommendations:
We recommended to the Local Chief Executive to:
Direct the OIC-Municipal Treasurer to explain in writing why such  accountable forms issued were not properly monitored and accounted for.
Require the persons who made an IOUs/Vales to refund the amount of P390,063.
Require the OIC-Municipal Treasurer to determine their average monthly usage of Accountable Forms and on the basis thereof request Accountable Forms in sufficient quantities not to exceed three months' use.
Instruct the OIC-Municipal Treasurer to issue the Accountable Forms in strict numerical sequence with approved Requisition Issue Voucher.
Cause the Revenue Collecting Clerk III to produce and account for the missing Accountable Forms.
Maintain a separate logbook for Accountable Forms and provide all the necessary details in strict observance of Section 95, Volume I of GAAM.
Prepare and submit religiously the Monthly Report of Accountable Forms in accordance with section 37 of COA Circular No. 92-382.

Recommendations:
We have recommended to the Local Chief Executive to:
a.) Institute appropriate administrative and/or criminal charges against the OIC-Municipal Treasurer and the Collecting Officer for malversation of public funds pursuant to Article 217 of the Revised Penal Code.
b.) Require the OIC-Municipal Treasurer as Head of the Treasury Office to explain in writing why issued accountable forms were kept for a long period of time and was not demanded for remittance or deposits by the collecting officer to the Authorized Depository Bank or her office as Municipal Treasurer.
c.) Direct the OIC-Municipal Treasurer to explain in writing why collecting officers were allowed to accept IOUs, vales or other forms of promissory notes out of the cash collections of the Municipality.
Demand from the Officers and Employees to refund the amount of IOUs/vales in the amount of P2,666,768 and if possible require the Municipal Budget Officer to appropriate and obligate the expenditures paid out from the collections.

Recommendations:
We recommended to the Local Chief Executive:
a.) 	Direct the OIC-Municipal Treasurer to require the Collecting Officers delegated with the task of collecting taxes and other revenues of the LGU to apply with the Bureau of Treasury a bond sufficient to cover their cash accountability.
b.)	Strictly prohibit to designate Collecting Officers and employees with Job Order Status to collect and  whom were not bonded.
c.) Review the plantilla position of Treasury Office and deploy personnel/staff with competence, computer literate, and possess high-degree of integrity and other core values, where trust and confidence can be relied upon.
d.)	Require the Municipal Budget Officer to provide budget for bond premium.

Recommendations:
We recommended the Local Chief Executive to:
Direct the OIC-Municipal Treasurer to liquidate immediately her unliquidated cash advances for payroll and operating expenses in the amount of P454,992.
Ensure that the Accountable Officers which were granted cash advance must liquidate their cash advances within the required period provided in item 5.1 of COA Circular No. 97-002 dated February 10, 1997.
Withheld the salary or any money payable to the OIC-Municipal Treasurer until the cash advances has been fully liquidated and settled pursuant to item 5.1 and 9.3.2 of COA Circular No. 97-002 dated February 10, 1997.
Recording of Liquidation should be made on a per cash advance basis and not on a monthly basis.
Record the ATM Payroll in a separate Subsidiary Ledger.
Maintain separate cashbooks for salaries, wages, allowances, etc and for operating expenses. The Accountable Officer shall record the transactions in the prescribed cashbook daily in accordance with Item 6 of COA Circular No. 97-002 dated February 10, 1997.

Recommendations:
We recommended to the Local Chief Executive the following:
Require the OIC-Municipal Treasurer and Collecting Officers to diligently and regularly update the posting of her cashbook; record collections and deposits on a daily basis for easy determination, monitoring and verification of balances.
Cause the Collecting Officer to maintain separate cashbook for each fund handled in accordance with what the National Government Accounting System Manual for LGUs prescribes.
Direct both the Municipal Accountant and the OIC-Municipal Treasurer for immediate reconciliation of their records at least quarterly.
Require the OIC- Municipal Treasurer to submit justification on the above-mentioned deficiencies.

Recommendation:
We recommended to the Local Chief Executive to refrain from entrusting and delegating collection of government funds and revenues to Job Order personnel, if cannot be done, the Municipal Treasurer shall take the risk of any loss that may derived therefrom.

Recommendations:
We have recommended to the Local Chief Executive the following:
To create an Inventory Committee to conduct complete physical inventory count of all PPE and submit Report on the Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment (RPCPPE).
If possible designate another permanent personnel to handle the General Services Office to cope with the requirements.
Instruct the General Services Officer/Supply Officer-Designate and the Municipal Accountant to regularly reconcile their records and review the composition of the PPE account to come up with an accurate Property, Plant and Equipment account balance.
Require the General Services Officer/Supply Officer-Designate to issue property tags/ stickers for each PPE in order to identify and trace the location of the said PPEs and to issue corresponding Acknowledgement Receipt for Equipment (ARE) for all the Properties, Plant and Equipment issued to officials and employees who have the custody thereto and to facilitate such undertaking, assign additional competent personnel in order to complete the needed property records.

Recommendations:
We recommended to Local Chief Executive to enjoin the following department heads in compliance with the requirements of COA Circular No.  2015-008:
Direct the Municipal Engineer to provide the Municipal Accountant, General Services Officer with the complete description and cost segregation of road components for road projects;
Direct the General Services Officer (Municipal Treasurer) to maintain the Local Road Inventory and Road Map, and to keep a complete Local Road Network Property Card for all roads and its components; and
Require the Municipal Accountant the following reporting procedures:
Prepare the Journal Voucher (JV) to record the beginning balance of the local road network and its components in the general ledger and the Local Road Network Ledger Card, respectively. Support the JV with the Registry of Public Infrastructures for Roads, working paper on the distribution of costs for the road components, and working paper on the determination of the depreciated replacement cost for road components with no available cost per registry.
Keep and maintain subsidiary records for roads and road components for every road network; and
Prepare a lapsing schedule for the computation of the depreciation for each component at the end of the year.

Recommendations:
We recommended to the Local Chief Executive to:
Direct the head of Human Resource Management Office to submit the contract of service of the job orders with specific duties and functions and reconcile the manpower complement based on the actual manpower and instruct to use the computer-generated DTRs based on the captured biometric date as basis for their preparation of payrolls for the establishment of a reliable number of days worked as the basis for the computation of their wages.
Comply with the aforementioned rules and regulations in the hiring of Job Orders, prepare and issue policies and guidelines to address the issue on the necessity and propriety of hiring JOs.
Immediately strengthen the controls in the hiring of job order by requiring them to submit personal records, undergo interview and document the evaluation of performance to give assurance that the risk identified above are addressed.
Consider either one of the following:
Appoint Job Order personnel as regular employees if they are qualified and believe that their services are essential and contributory to the improvement of agency’s operations or,
Stop hiring Job Order and fill up the vacant plantilla positions in accordance with the Civil Service Commission rules and regulations. Prioritize the division with undermanned in the filling-up of vacant plantilla position.
e.) Formulate a policy and internal control in the hiring of job order workers such as but not limited to requiring specific and detailed description of duties and   responsibilities to eliminate working with having no definite purpose, if any.
f.) 	Require the Local Chief Executive to explain in writing the excessive expenditure compared to its budget, and to submit supplemental budget, if any. Otherwise, it shall be disallowed in audit.

Recommendations:
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive to:
a.) Establish a system in the withdrawal of fuel, oil and lubricants from the LGU to supplier such as:
i.) The driver will submit a requisition/request form duly numbered for approval by the General Services Officer and attach the duly filled up Drivers Trip Tickets.
ii.)	Require the Supplier to submit Statement of Account (SOA) duly supported with the Charge Invoices.
b.) The GSO should designate a personnel to prepare the Monthly Report of Official Travels and the Monthly Report of Fuel Consumption for submission to the Municipal Accountant.
c.) 	Compel the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) to conduct public bidding in the procurement of fuel, oil and lubricants. Otherwise, other mode of procurement will be properly recommended by the BAC.
d.) The Municipal Accountant should refrain from processing of claims without the above-mentioned documents.

Recommendations:
We recommended to the Local Chief Executive to:
Direct the Municipal Accountant to immediately strengthen the controls in the payment of terminal leave benefits to give assurance that the risk identified above is addressed.
Require the Human Resource Management Officer to prepare a summary of leave credit of all employees at the end of the year for employees’ information and submit to the Office of Municipal Accountant;
Direct the Municipal Budget Officer, Municipal Accountant and Human Resource Management Officer to explain the cause of the overpayment of terminal leave benefits, and require the payee to make a refund on the excess claim.
Strictly adhere to the Omnibus Rules on Leave and be judicious and frugal in its expenses in consonance with COA Circular 2012-003.

Recommendation:
We have recommended to require the General Services Officer-Designate and the Municipal Accountant to maintain Stock and Property Cards for Supplies, and Property, Plant and Equipment Ledger Cards, respectively. The required records shall also be the basis by the GSO for its reconciliation with the actual physical inventory.

Recommendations:
We recommended to Local Chief Executive to:
a.) Direct the Municipal Treasurer to submit disbursement vouchers after payment with complete supporting documents to the Office of Municipal Accountant.
b.) Direct the Municipal Accountant to strictly adhere to the prescribed time frame for the submission of monthly and quarterly reports as well as transmitting all receipts and disbursement records with complete supporting documents to the Office of the Auditor.
c.) Direct the Municipal Accountant to closely coordinate with the Municipal Treasurer for the latter to submit immediately the required reports within the prescribed period, and to facilitate the recording of the transactions in the books of accounts.
d.) Request the Local Chief Executive and the Municipal Treasurer to enroll in LBP i-access so that bank balances can be viewed and printed anytime.

Recommendations:
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive to:
Instruct the Bids and Awards Committee to properly plan the procurement of goods and supplies and strictly observe the Implementing Rules and Regulation on RA No. 9184 or the Procurement Law. Competitive Bidding should be the primary mode of procurement in compliance with RA 9184 to promote transparency, economy and efficiency in the procurement process.
Require the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to draw check payments directly in the name of the supplier in conformity with existing regulations and withhold the appropriate taxes due the government.

Recommendation:
We recommended to the Local Chief Executive to direct the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) Secretariat to comply with the regulations and furnish the Audit Team copies of the contract agreements together with the corresponding supporting documents within the prescribed period for the timely review, evaluation and inspection of the same.

Recommendation:
We recommended to the Local Chief Executive to require the GAD Focal Person to prepare and submit the corresponding GAD Accomplishment Report within the period prescribed under COA Circular No. 2014-001 dated March 18, 2014.

Recommendations:
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive to:
Direct the focal point person to spearhead the establishment of GAD database to serve as basis for gender-responsive planning, programming, and policy formulation which will form part of the overall management information system of the agency.
Establish a GAD monitoring and evaluation (M&E) system as required under Section 5.1 of Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-01.

Recommendations:
We recommended to the Local Chief Executive to:
Direct the Municipal Planning and Development with the Engineering     department to plan in carrying out accurate design and effective production of the projects in order to ensure immediate implementation when the funds are available, and
Instruct the heads of the implementing offices to expedite the implementation of these projects to provide the expected benefits to the intended beneficiaries on time and to act promptly on issues or constraints causing the delay of the implementation of the projects.
Require the Municipal Engineering Office to explain in writing as to the cause of delay in the implementation of the projects.
Require the Local Chief Executive through the Municipal Engineer to file and demand payment for liquidated damages for the number of days delayed. If not, issue a Blacklisting Order disqualifying the erring contractor from participating in the bidding of all government projects.

Recommendations:
We recommended to the Local Chief Executive to:
Require the proponents headed by Municipal Planning and Development with the Engineering department to conduct thorough planning in coming up with timely implementation of proposed projects.
Instruct the heads of the implementing offices to prioritize and expedite the implementation of these projects to provide the expected benefits to the intended beneficiaries on time and maximum use of the programs/projects, and to act promptly on issues or constraints causing the non-implementation of the projects.
Direct the Head of the Agency to submit justification as to the cause of non-implementation of the above-mentioned projects.
Require the Municipal Accountant to return the unused amount of fund transfer or dormant accounts to the source agency.

Recommendation:
We recommended to the Local Chief Executive and to the Members of the Local School Board to closely monitor and strategically plan and implement the approved programs, projects and activities under the Special Education Fund and maximize the utilization of SEF to attain its objectives for the benefit of the constituents.

observations.recommendation:
Recommendations:
We recommended to the Local Chief Executive the following:
Cause OIC-Municipal Treasurer for the immediate full restitution of the missing funds.
Direct the persons who made an IOUs/Vales to refund the amount.
Institute appropriate administrative and/or criminal charges against the Accountable Officer for malversation of public funds pursuant to Article 217 of the Revised Penal Code.
Require Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant to reconcile their balances recorded in their books of accounts at least quarterly, and to submit reconciliation statement to the Audit Team for verification.
Require the Municipal Treasurer to install internal control measures to ensure that all collection be remitted/deposited intact and daily.

Management Comment:
The OIC-Municipal Treasurer promised to restitute the missing funds and to all concerned persons who made an IOUs/Vales to refund the amount.
The Local Chief Executive and concerned employees committed to comply with the other recommendations.
Auditor’s Rejoinder:
Considering the length of time, the Accountable Officer failed to fully restitute the amount of cash shortage, and the Management failed to institute legal actions/remedies to recover the amount lost.

Recommendations:
We recommended to the Local Chief Executive the following:
Cause the Accountable Officer for the immediate full restitution of the missing funds.
Direct the persons who made an IOUs/Vales to refund the amount.
Institute appropriate administrative and/or criminal charges against the Accountable Officer for malversation of public funds pursuant to Article 217 of the Revised Penal Code.

Management Comment:
The Local Chief Executive assures in doing follow-ups to the Accountable Officer to get the full restitution of the missing funds.
Auditor’s Rejoinder:
Due to the lapse of time, the Accountable Officer failed to fully restitute the amount of cash shortage, and the Management failed to institute legal actions/remedies to recover the amount lost.

Recommendations:
We recommended to the Local Chief Executive to:
Direct the OIC-Municipal Treasurer to explain in writing why such  accountable forms issued were not properly monitored and accounted for.
Require the persons who made an IOUs/Vales to refund the amount.
Require the OIC-Municipal Treasurer to determine their average monthly usage of Accountable Forms and on the basis thereof request Accountable Forms in sufficient quantities not to exceed three months' use.
Instruct the OIC-Municipal Treasurer to issue the Accountable Forms in strict numerical sequence with approved Requisition Issue Voucher.
Cause the Revenue Collecting Clerk III to produce and account for the missing Accountable Forms
Maintain a separate logbook for Accountable Forms and provide all the necessary details in strict observance of Section 95, Volume I of GAAM.
Prepare and submit religiously the Monthly Report of Accountable Forms in accordance with section 37 of COA Circular No. 92-382.

Management Comment:
The new Municipal Treasurer will adopt the audit recommendations taken up by the team.
Auditor’s Rejoinder:
Management should established strong internal control in the Municipal Treasurer’s Office.

Recommendations:
We have recommended to the Local Chief Executive to:
a.) Institute appropriate administrative and/or criminal charges against the Accountable Officer for malversation of public funds pursuant to Article 217 of the Revised Penal Code.
b.) 	Require the OIC-Municipal Treasurer as Head of the Treasury Office to explain in writing why issued accountable forms were kept for a long period of time and was not demanded for remittance or deposits by the collecting officer to the Authorized Depository Bank or her office as Municipal Treasurer.
c.) Direct the OIC-Municipal Treasurer to explain in writing why collecting officers were allowed to accept IOUs, vales or other forms of promissory notes out of the cash collections of the Municipality.
Demand from the Officers and Employees to refund the amount of IOUs/vales and require the Municipal Budget Officer to appropriate and obligate the expenditures paid out from the collections.

Management Comment:
The management will seek legal opinion as to the degree of culpability of the persons involve so as to derive a strong case in order to avoid waste of financial and time resources in the process.
Auditor’s Rejoinder:
Of the total amount of P3,056,830 that were cash advanced by LGU Officers and Employees thru accomodation of IOUs/Vales, only P43,824 were refunded and deposited in the bank, thus, the amount of P3,013,006 remained unsettled as indicated in the status report.Z This indicates leniency of the Management to take immediate actions on this matter.
Also, there were some personnel who made a reply on the Demand Letter stating that they already liquidated the vales by submitting the official receipts to the OIC-Municipal Treasurer, but allegedly the later failed to returned back their IOUs/vales. Nonetheless, there was no proof of any liquidation/receipt that was duly received by the OIC-Municipal Treasurer.
There might be a misinterpretation on the cash advances as recorded in the books of accounts which were processed in the accounting office thru disbursement voucher and were liquidated thru liquidation reports. The IOUs/Vales pertains to borrowing of funds taken outrightly from the collections in the Treasurer’s Office which violates existing rules and regulations and unremained unsettled as of this time.

Recommendations:
We recommended to the Local Chief Executive:
a.) 	Direct the OIC-Municipal Treasurer to require the Collecting Officers delegated with the task of collecting taxes and other revenues of the LGU to apply with the Bureau of Treasury a bond sufficient to cover their cash accountability.
b.)	Employees with Job Order Status and other Collecting Officers who were not bonded should not be allowed to collect.
c.)	Review the plantilla position of Treasury Office and deploy personnel/staff with competence, computer literate, and possess high-degree of integrity and other core values, where trust and confidence can be relied upon.
d.)	Require the Municipal Budget Officer to provide budget for bond premium.

Management Comment:
The Accountable Officers has already posted fidelity bond to the Bureau of Treasury. One (1) collector of Market & Terminal is recommended to be permanent so he can be instituted bond.

Recommendations:
We recommended the Local Chief Executive to:
Cause the OIC-Municipal Treasurer to liquidate immediately her unliquidated cash advances for payroll and operating expenses.
Ensure that the Accountable Officers which were granted cash advance must liquidate their cash advances within the required period provided in item 5.1 of COA Circular No. 97-002 dated February 10, 1997.
Withheld the salary or any money payable to the OIC-Municipal Treasurer until the cash advances has been fully liquidated and settled pursuant to item 5.1 and 9.3.2 of COA Circular No. 97-002 dated February 10, 1997.
Recording of Liquidation should be made on a per cash advance basis and not on a monthly basis.
Record the ATM Payroll in a separate Subsidiary Ledger.
Maintain separate cashbooks for salaries, wages, allowances, etc and for operating expenses. The Accountable Officer shall record the transactions in the prescribed cashbook daily in accordance with Item 6 of COA Circular No. 97-002 dated February 10, 1997.

Management Comment:
The Local Chief Executive and the concerned employees will adopt the audit recommendations.

Cause the Collecting Officer to maintain separate cashbook for each fund handled in accordance with what the National Government Accounting System Manual for LGUs prescribes.
Direct both the Municipal Accountant and the OIC-Municipal Treasurer for immediate reconciliation of their records at least quarterly.

Consider either one of the following:

Stop hiring Job Order and fill up the vacant plantilla positions in accordance with the Civil Service Commission rules and regulations. Prioritize the division with undermanned in the filling-up of vacant plantilla position.
c.) 	Formulate a policy and internal control in the hiring of job order workers such as but not limited to requiring specific and detailed description of duties and responsibilities to eliminate workings having no definite purpose, if any.

Recommendations:

a.) Direct the Municipal Treasurer to submit disbursement vouchers after payment with complete supporting documents to the Office of Municipal Accountant.
b.) Direct the Municipal Accountant to strictly adhere to the prescribed time frame for the submission of monthly and quarterly reports as well as transmitting all receipts and disbursement records with complete supporting documents to the Office of the Auditor.
c.) Direct the Municipal Accountant to closely coordinate with the Municipal         Treasurer for the latter to submit immediately the required reports within the prescribed period, and to facilitate the recording of the transactions in the books of accounts.

Management Comment:
The Management explained that the delayed submission of disbursement vouchers, official receipt and other reports were caused by the following factors, viz: (1) Late submission and incomplete paid disbursement vouchers and official receipts submitted to the accounting office from the treasurer’s office; (2) Only one bookkeeper in charge to all funds, thus, the recording is slow unless the treasurer will be on time in the transmission of the said disbursement vouchers and official receipts; (3) Bank reconciliation shall be done only if the bank statement is received from the bank/mailed by the bank; (4) Only one person in charge ti do the monthly bank reconciliation to all 17 bank accounts; (5) The accounting office nees more competent personnel to fast track the work due to the increase volume of documents and reports submission; (6) Year-end reports were submitted on time; and (7) The distance of COA is far and the Auditor has no office in the agency for fast submission of paid disbursement vouchers, official receipts, and other reports.
