observations.recommendation:
We recommended the following:

Require the Municipal Treasurer to diligently and regularly update the posting of her cashbook;

Direct both the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer for immediate reconciliation of their records, if not, demand the Municipal Treasurer to reinstitute the amount of P951,933.52; and

Cause the Municipal Treasurer to submit justification on the above-mentioned deficiencies.
observations.managementAction: Instruct the Municipal Treasurer to update collection and cashbook, properly record the transactions to avoid errors and judicially follow the guidelines of COA Circular No. 97-002. Moreover, Municipal Accountant verbally instructed MTO and bookkeeper to reconcile the records.
observations.reason:
(Reiterated in this 2018 AAR) Finding No. 7 | The previous OIC-Municipal Treasurer failed to properly maintained a cashbook.

The newly installed Municipal Treasurer kept a new separate cashbook for each bank account, collections and cash advances.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended to the Local Chief Executive to:

Direct the Municipal Treasurer to explain in writing why such accountable forms issued were not properyl monitored and accounted for.

Require the Municipal Treasurer to determine their average monthly usage of Accountable Forms and on the basis thereof request Accountable Forms in sufficient quantities not to exceed three months' use.

The Municipal Treasurer should issue the Accountable Forms in strict numerical sequence and upon a Requisition Issue Voucher.

Cause the Revenue Collecting Clerk II to produce and account for the missing Accountable Forms. If the Missing Accountable Forms were lost, require the Municipal Treasurer to request for relief from accountability pursuant to COA Rules and Regulations. 

Maintain a separate logbook for Accountable Forms and provide all the necessary details in strict observance of Section 95, Volume I of GAAM.

Prepare and submit religiously the Monthly Report of Accountable Forms in accordance with section 37 of COA Circular No. 92-382.
observations.managementAction: Collecting Officer should be guided accordingly and be brief as per GAAM to avoid loss and misuse of accountable forms
observations.reason: (Reiterated in this 2018 AAR) Finding No. 3 | The OIC-Municipal Treasurer explained that issued official receipts were already liquidated and reported. Also, reconciliation of accountable forms were submitted for auditors verification.

observations.recommendation:
We have recommended the following:

The Local Chief Executive should create an Inventory Committee to conduct complete physical inventory count of all PPE and submit Report on the Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment (RPCPPE). 

If possible designate another personnel to handle the General Services Office to cope with the requirements. 
	Instruct the General Services Officer/Supply Officer and the Municipal Accountant to regularly reconcile their records and review the composition of the PPE account to come up with an accurate Property, Plant and Equipment account balance.
observations.managementAction:
Assigned personnel to conduct Physical Inventory count of all properties. 
Then, property tagging will be done once all documents will be reconciled with the accounting office.
observations.reason: (Reiterated in this 2018 AAR) Finding No. 9 | On-going reconciliation of PPE account, but, due to lack of manpower and time constraints hinders the timely submission of the inventory report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended to the Local Chief Executive to secure legal basis in the granting of honorarium and other allowances to other employees stationed at the Municipality. And submit it to the Audit Team for evaluation.
observations.managementAction: The Local Chief Executive directed that in every transaction made in the municipality, supporting documents should be required.
observations.reason: The Municipality had stopped the payment of allowances and honorarium. However, those issued with disallowance failed to refund the amount.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Local Chief Executive to require the Municipal Accountant to 	implement the P4,000 net take home pay required for each employee as provided under 	the General Provisions of the General Appropriation  	Act (GAA) for FY 2017 also known as Republic Act No. 10924 in order for the 	employees to work efficiently on the assigned job.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality will implement the audit finding.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended to the Local Chief Executive to:

Direct the Municipality’s officers and employees to impose proper application of the entitlement of traveling allowance as well as completeness and regularity of the essential documents like travel orders, bus tickets, e-ticket, boarding pass and itinerary of travel. 

b.) Require the concerned officials and employees to refund the excess amount of the travelling expenses claimed by the officials.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality will strictly implement the provisions of EO No. 298.
observations.reason: The Municipality had implemented the provisions of EO No. 298. However, those issued with disallowance failed to fully refund the amount.

observations.recommendation: We recommended to direct the General Services Office (GSO) - Designate to prioritize procurement of regular or ordinary office supplies first from PS-DBM.  Shopping from private suppliers should be resorted to only if the items being purchased are not available in the Procurement Service Depot. The Agency Procurement Request (APR) duly acted by the PS-DBM and the Price Quotation and Stocks Availability Certification must be attached in the disbursement vouchers.
observations.managementAction: The Local Chief Executive instructed GSO to require all approved purchased request to BAC for deliberation. The lowest price for the government will be attained and mode of procurement was emphasized.
observations.reason:
Not all procurement for commonly-used office supplies were procured at PS-DBM. 

The Municipality still adopt “shopping” or small value  procurement as alternative mode of procurement.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended to Local Chief Executive to:

a.) Direct the Municipal Treasurer to submit disbursement vouchers after payment with complete supporting documents to the Office of Municipal Accountant. 
b.) Direct the Municipal Accountant to strictly adhere to the prescribed time frame for the submission of monthly and quarterly reports as well as transmitting all receipts and disbursement records with complete supporting documents to the Office of the Auditor. 
c.) Direct the Municipal Accountant to closely coordinate with the Municipal Accountant to the Municipal Treasurer for the latter to submit immediately the required reports within the prescribed period, and to facilitate the recording of the transactions in the books of accounts.
observations.managementAction: Demand letters sent to Municipal Treasurer’s Office for the submission of DV’s and OR’s
observations.reason: (Reiterated in this 2018 AAR) Finding No. 13 | Late submission of disbursement vouchers and supporting documents from the Municipal Treasurer’s Office.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended the following:

Direct the Local Chief Executive to strictly adhere with the provisions of RA 7160 or Local Government Code of 1991 in the preparation and submission of the local budget, supplemental budget, realignment, and approved legislative resolutions.
 
Require the Municipal Budget Officer to prepare the quarterly Statement of Appropriation, Allotments, Obligations and Balances (SAAOB) and to submit the same to the audit team.
observations.managementAction: The Local Chief Executive emphasized the Municipal Budget Offcier the importance of the annual budget preparation, copy of the approved budget will be submitted to COA office.
observations.reason: The Municipal Budget Officer had submitted the Municipal Annual Budget. However, the related SAAOB were not submitted quarterly.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that the Local Chief Executive should direct the Municipal Budget Officer to conform with the rules on the use and prioritization 	of savings from Personal Services and comply with the regulation on the 	realignment and augmentation of budgetary item within the allotment class. 	Otherwise, the Audit Team will issue Notice of Disallowance.
observations.managementAction: The LGU will take note of the audit finding and recommendation.
observations.reason: The SB still issued resolution for the realignment out from savings of PS to MOOE.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended to the Local Chief Executive to:

Direct the Department Heads to prepare and submit their individual Project Procurement Management Plan (PPMP) showing monthly breakdown for consolidation to the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC).

BAC Secretariat shall prepare the Annual Procurement Plan to guide the agency in the procurement of goods and services, and strictly monitor compliance with the Implementing Rules and Regulations of RA 9184.
observations.managementAction: The Local Chief Executive advised to submit their Annual Procurement Plan to ensure procurements are in accordance with priority programs.

observations.recommendation:
We have recommended the following:

Strictly compel the Local Chief Executive to abide the Implementing Rules and Regulation on RA No. 9184 or the Procurement Law.

Direct the Bids and Awards Committee to adopt competitive bidding in the procurement process and comply the conditions on the use of the alternative mode of procurement.

Require the Municipal Accountant and the Supply Officer/General Services Officer Designate to refrain the processing of liquidation reports for the above-mentioned disbursements because the above disbursements may be suspended or disallowed in audit.
observations.managementAction: Shopping was practiced for a couple of years now, BAC now are currently adapting the RA 9184.
observations.reason: (Reiterated in this 2018 AAR) Finding No. 14 | Mostly, the Municipality still adopt “shopping” or small value procurement as mode of procurement in its transactions.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended to the Local Chief Executive to:

a.) Direct the Local Development Council to optimized the utilization of the 20% Development Fund through proper planning of project including timing of execution; coordinated action from LGU Officials for better result; and strict monitoring in project implementation to ensure accomplishment of the project.

b.) Ensure that expenditures should have appropriation in the Annual Budget. Otherwise, the Audit Team will issue Notice of Disallowance.
observations.managementAction: The Chief Executive emphasized to utilized the 20% development fund to projects for socio-economic and environmental development beneficial to constituents.
observations.reason: (Reiterated in this 2018 AAR) Finding No. 20 |

observations.recommendation: We recommended to the Local Chief Executive to require the concerned LGU officials to prepare and submit a copy of the DILG approved annual GAD Plan and Budget (GPB) and the corresponding GAD Accomplishment Report within the period prescribed under COA Circular No. 2014-001 dated March 18, 2014.
observations.managementAction: Assigned GAD Focal person to submit a copy og DILG approved plan budget as mandated by Memorandum Circular No. 2013-01.
observations.reason: The LGU GAD Focal Point (GFP) did not submit the 2017 GAD Accomplishment Report, only the CY2018 report was submitted.

observations.recommendation: We recommended to direct the Municipal Treasurer to strictly comply rules and regulations in the handling and custody of cash collections, and demand immediate deposits of the remaining undeposited amount.
observations.managementAction: Adopt audit recommendation
observations.reason: The undeposited collection increased to P1,290,148 due to payment of expenses out of the collection.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the Municipal Accountant to comply with and observe strictly to the provision of COA Circular No. 97-002. If the cash advances remained outstanding for more than a month after the completion of its purpose, demand letters should be sent out requiring the officials/employees to submit an accounting or liquidation thereof within seventy-two (72) hours. Otherwise, the whole amount should be deducted from his/her salary.
observations.managementAction: Adopt audit recommendation
observations.reason: The account increased to P4,019,323 despited issuance of demand letters to official and employees.

observations.recommendation:
We have recommended the following:

The Local Chief Executive should refrain from granting of cash advances to Elective Officials other than their traveling expenses.
Direct the Municipal Accountant to send demand letters for immediate liquidation of the outstanding balance.

Direct the Municipal Accountant to strictly require the submission of the liquidation report of the previous cash advances before another cash advances shall be granted.
observations.managementAction: Adopt audit recommendation
observations.reason: The account increased to P968,747 despite of issuance of demand letter by the Municipal Accountant.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Municipal Treasurer and Collecting Officers to strictly comply the submission of the monthly and consolidated report of accountability for accountable forms.
observations.managementAction: Adopt audit recommendation
observations.reason: The Municipal Treasurer explained that they overlook the preparation of the RAAF and commits to comply the monthly submission of RAAF.

observations.recommendation: We recommended to require the LGU GAD Focal Point (GFP) to submit timely the GAD Accomplishment Report as illustrated under Annexes of the PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA Joint Circular No. 20013-01.
observations.managementAction: Adopt audit recommendation
observations.reason: The LGU GAD Focal Point (GFP) did not submit the report.

observations.recommendation:
Recommendations:
We have recommended to the Local Chief Executive to:
File administrative and criminal charges against the collecting officer for malversation of funds, for failure to produce the missing funds and failure to act on the letter of demand issued by the Audit Team.
Require the Municipal Treasurer as head of the Treasury Office to explain in writing why issued accountable forms were kept for a long period of time and was not demanded for remittance or deposits by the collecting officer to the Authorized Depository Bank or her office as Municipal Treasurer.
Also, require the Municipal Treasurer to explain in writing why collecting officers were allowed to accept IOUs, vales or other forms of promissory notes out of the cash collections of the Municipality.

Recommendations:
We recommended to the Local Chief Executive to:
Direct the accountable officer to remit collections intact daily in consonance with Section 69 of P.D. 1445; and the Municipal Treasurer to strictly adhere the provision of COA Circular 92-382 dated July 3, 1992 to avoid possible misappropriation of asset;
Demand immediate deposits of the remaining undeposited amount;
Stop payment of expenses out of the cash collections and comply with the COA rules and regulations pertaining to it.
Management Comment:
Collections in the amount of P557,739.63 was deposited on January 4, 2018. Typhoon Vinta expenses in the amount of P66,215.52 was partially liquidated. Delayed in deposit was due to weather condition and holidays.
The Local Chief Executive and Municipal Treasurer committed to comply with the recommendations and will issue the necessary memo’s regarding to these effect.

Recommendations:
We have recommended to the Local Chief Executive to:
The Municipal Treasurer’s Office should intensify its effort to collect sufficient funds in the succeeding year to cover the cash overdraft.
The Local Chief Executive should adopt austerity measures to avoid incurrence of cash overdraft in its future operations.
Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant to regularly remit all trust liabilities to the agencies concerned to avoid utilization of such funds for other purposes.
Management Comment:
The cause of incurring overdraft was due to budget deficits on loans payable where the quarterly amortization directly debited to our bank account. The Municipal Mayor has started adopting austerity measures.

Recommendations:
We have recommended to the Local Chief Executive the following:
Compel the LGU officials and employees to liquidate their cash advances otherwise, the non-liquidation of the cash advances granted constitute for the cause of filing of malversation of funds under the Revised Penal Code and appropriate administrative proceedings;
Direct the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to refrain from granting of cash advances to elective officials other than their travelling expenses.
c.) Direct the LGU officials and employees to abide with the implementing rules and regulations on Republic Act 9184 or the Procurement Law because COA has conducted seminars in 2017.
d.) Direct the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to stop the granting of cash advances unless the previous cash advances were liquidated.
e.) 	Require the Municipal Accountant to issued letter of demand to officers and employees for immediate liquidation of the outstanding balance.
Management Comment:
During the exit conference, the Municipal Mayor agreed to abide with the IRR of RA 9184, to follow the rules in the granting of cash advances and the Municipal Accountant promised to issued letter of demand to those with unliquidated cash advances.
Rejoinder:
The LGU officials continued such practice and instead of improved performance, the LGU has increased its cash advances as compared to past years. Same scenario was obtained considering that this is a reiteration of prior year’s recommendation.

Recommendations:
We have recommended to the Local Chief Executive the following:
Strictly require the LGU officials and employees to liquidate their cash advanes otherwise, the non-liquidation of the cash advances granted constitute for the cause of filing of malversation of funds under the Revised Penal Code and appropriate administrative proceedings;
Direct the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to stop the granting of cash advances unless the previous cash advances were liquidated.
Require the Municipal Accountant to issued letter of demand to officers and employees for immediate liquidation of the outstanding balance.
Management Comment:
The Municipal Accountant issued demand letters those official and employees with unliquidated cash advances.

Recommendation:
We recommended the following:
Require the Personnel Department to meticulously check the individual daily time record of its employees, and immediate preparation of the payroll for approval of the Head of Office.
Direct the Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant to check the completeness of the payroll and its supporting documents before payment is made.
Close coordination with the Personnel Department, Accounting Office and Treasury Office is sought for improvement of the Municipality’s operations.
Management Comment:
The Municipality already adopted and implemented the audit recommendation by requiring job order employees to submit Daily Time Record and accomplishment report. Also, require the Municipal Treasurer to duly process the disbursement voucher before payment be made.

Recommendation:
We recommended to the Local Chief Executive:
To direct the Municipal Treasurer to refrain from issuing checks without insufficient fund, in compliance with Section 344 of RA 7160.
To direct the Municipal Treasurer’s Office to adopt appropriate measures to control not only the appropriations, allotments but also the cash availability as well to safeguard its assets and check the accuracy and reliability of its data.
To refund the bank charges as a result of drawing checks with insufficient fund.
To explain in writing why she should not administratively charged for intentionally drawing checks with insufficient fund.
Management Comment:
The Municipal Treasurer will enroll in iAccess with Land Bank of the Philippines (LBP) to monitor the bank balances and will not draw anymore checks to avoid shortage.

Recommendations:
We recommended the following:
Require the Municipal Treasurer to diligently and regularly update the posting of her cashbook;
Direct both the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer for immediate reconciliation of their records, if not, demand the Municipal Treasurer to reinstitute the amount of P951,933.52; and
Cause the Municipal Treasurer to submit justification on the above-mentioned deficiencies.
Management Comment:
The Municipal Treasurer has updated her cashbook. Reconciliation of cash advances is on process and partially liquidated. Instructions were given to the Bookkeeper and the Treasurer to reconcile and provided a copy of subsidiary ledger for reconciliation.

Recommendations:
We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer:
Cause the Collecting Officer to update the cashbook and record collections and deposits on a daily basis for easy determination, monitoring and verification of balances;
Cause the Collecting Officer to maintain separate cashbook for each fund handled in accordance with what the National Government Accounting System Manual for LGUs prescribes.
Management Comment:
The Collecting Officer of Maigo Water Utilities has already maintained cashbook. The cashbook for general fund and other funds shall be continued and be kept updated.

Recommendations:
We recommended to the Local Chief Executive to:
Direct the Municipal Treasurer to explain in writing why such accountable forms issued were not properyl monitored and accounted for.
Require the Municipal Treasurer to determine their average monthly usage of Accountable Forms and on the basis thereof request Accountable Forms in sufficient quantities not to exceed three months' use.
The Municipal Treasurer should issue the Accountable Forms in strict numerical sequence with approved Requisition Issue Voucher.
Cause the Revenue Collecting Clerk II to produce and account for the missing Accountable Forms. If the Missing Accountable Forms were lost, require the Municipal Treasurer to request for relief from accountability pursuant to COA Rules and Regulations.
Maintain a separate logbook for Accountable Forms and provide all the necessary details in strict observance of Section 95, Volume I of GAAM.
Prepare and submit religiously the Monthly Report of Accountable Forms in accordance with section 37 of COA Circular No. 92-382.
Management Comment:
The Municipal Treasurer will adopt the audit recommendation.

Recommendations:
We recommended to the Local Chief Executive:
a.) Direct the Municipal Treasurer to require the Accountable Officers delegated with the task of collecting taxes and other revenues of the LGU to apply with the Bureau of Treasury a bond sufficient to cover their cash accountability.
b.)	Direct the Collecting Officer to refrain from delegating collection of government funds and revenues to employees who are not bonded as well as those Job Order employees.
Management Comment:
The Accountable Officers has already posted fidelity bond to the Bureau of Treasury. The two (2) collectors of Market & Terminal are recommended to be permanent so they can be instituted bond.

Recommendation:
We have recommended the following:
The Local Chief Executive should create an Inventory Committee to conduct complete physical inventory count of all PPE and submit Report on the Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment (RPCPPE).
If possible designate another permanent personnel to handle the General Services Office to cope with the requirements.
Instruct the General Services Officer/Supply Officer and the Municipal Accountant to regularly reconcile their records and review the composition of the PPE account to come up with an accurate Property, Plant and Equipment account balance.
Management Comment:
A committee for the inventory were already created and memorandum order shall be submitted. The Supply Officer submit the PPE and RPCPPE. The accounting office has assigned one (1) personnel in charge in the property and equipment for inventory and reconcile with the records of the GSO.

Recommendation:
We recommended to require the General Services Officer-Designate and the Municipal Accountant to maintain Stock and Property Cards for Supplies, and Property,  Plant and Equipment Ledger Cards, respectively. The required records shall also be the basis by the GSO for its reconciliation with the actual physical inventory.
Management Comment:
The Management has assigned personnel to prepare and maintain the property ledger cards and stock cards.

Recommendation:
We recommended to the Local Chief Executive to secure legal basis in the granting of honorarium and other allowances to other employees stationed at the Municipality. And submit it to the Audit Team for evaluation.
Management Comment:
Memorandum signed by the Local Chief Executive (LCE) as basis for authorizing to receive allowances shall be submitted to the COA and/or attached to the disbursement voucher.

Recommendation:
We recommended that Local Chief Executive to require the Municipal  Accountant to 	implement the P4,000 net take home pay required for each  	employee as provided under  	the General Provisions of the General Appropriation  	Act (GAA) for FY 2017 also known  as Republic Act No. 10924 in order for the  	employees to work efficiently on the assigned job.
Management Comment:
Instructions were given to the personnel in-charge on the preparation of the monthly payroll to follow Republic Act No. 10924.

Recommendations:
We recommended to the Local Chief Executive to:
Direct the Municipality’s officers and employees to impose proper application of the entitlement of traveling allowance as well as completeness and regularity of the essential documents like travel orders, bus tickets, e-ticket, boarding pass and itinerary of travel.
b.)	Require the concerned officials and employees to refund the excess amount of the travelling expenses claimed by the officials.
Management Comment:
The Municipality will adopt the audit recommendation and to strictly adhere the Executive Order No. 298.

Recommendation:
We recommended to direct the General Services Office (GSO) - Designate to prioritize procurement of regular or ordinary office supplies first from PS-DBM.  Shopping from private suppliers should be resorted to only if the items being procured are not available in the Procurement Service Depot. The Agency Procurement Request (APR) duly acted by the PS-DBM and the Price Quotation and Stocks Availability Certification must be attached in the disbursement vouchers.
Management Comment:
The process is costly and the distance between the PS-DBM and the agency is so far. It entails large amount of travelling expenses in going to and from in the purchase process.

Recommendations:
We recommended to Local Chief Executive to:
a.) Direct the Municipal Treasurer to submit disbursement vouchers after payment with complete supporting documents to the Office of Municipal Accountant.
b.) Direct the Municipal Accountant to strictly adhere to the prescribed time frame for the submission of monthly and quarterly reports as well as transmitting all receipts and disbursement records with complete supporting documents to the Office of the Auditor.
c.) Direct the Municipal Accountant to closely coordinate with the Municipal Treasurer for the latter to submit immediately the required reports within the prescribed period, and to facilitate the recording of the transactions in the books of accounts.
Management Comment:
The delayed submissiopm of disbursement vouchers, official receipt and other reports caused by the following factors, viz: (1) Late submission and incomplete paid disbursement vouchers and official receipts submitted to the accounting office from the treasurer’s office; (2) Only one bookkeeper in charge to all funds, thus, the recording is slow unless the treasurer will be on time in the transmission of the said disbursement vouchers and official receipts; (3) Bank reconciliation shall be done only if the bank statement is received from the bank/mailed by the bank; (4) Only one person in charge ti do the monthly bank reconciliation to all 17 bank accounts; (5) The accounting office nees more competent personnel to fast track the work due to the increase volume of documents and reports submission; (6) Year-end reports were submitted on time; and (7) The distance of COA is far and the Auditor has no office in the agency for fast submission of paid disbursement vouchers, official receipts, and other reports.

Recommendation:
We recommended the following:
Direct the Local Chief Executive to strictly adhere with the provisions of RA 7160 or Local Government Code of 1991 in the preparation and submission of the local budget, supplemental budget, realignment, and approved legislative resolutions.
Require the Municipal Budget Officer to prepare the quarterly Statement of Appropriation, Allotments, Obligations and Balances (SAAOB) and to submit the same to the audit team.
Management Comment:
The delayed approval of the Annual Investment Plan (AIP) from the Office of Municipal Planning and Development Council (MPDC) caused the delayed submission of the Annual Budget. The budget will not be reviewed unless an AIP be incorporated in the Annual Budget.

Recommendation:
We have recommended that the Local Chief Executive should direct the  Municipal Budget Officer to conform with the rules on the use and  	prioritization 	of savings from Personal Services and comply with the regulation 	on the 	realignment and augmentation of budgetary item within the allotment class. 	Otherwise, the Audit Team will issue Notice of Disallowance.
Management Comment:
There was realignment of savings since the Municipality has no enough appropriation for the payment of wages for the job orders and travelling expenses.

Recommendations:
We recommended to the Local Chief Executive to:
Direct the Department Heads to prepare and submit their individual Project Procurement Management Plan (PPMP) showing monthly breakdown for consolidation to the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC).
BAC Secretariat shall prepare the Annual Procurement Plan to guide the agency in the procurement of goods and services, and strictly monitor compliance with the Implementing Rules and Regulations of RA 9184.
Management Comment:
Project Procurement Management Plan (PPMP) were submitted for consolidation and copy of the Annual Procurement Plan (APP) shall be submitted to the COA audit team.

Recommendations:
We have recommended the following:
Strictly compel the Local Chief Executive to abide the Implementing Rules and Regulation on RA No. 9184 or the Procurement Law.
Direct the Bids and Awards Committee to adopt competitive bidding in the procurement process and comply the conditions on the use of the alternative mode of procurement.
Require the Municipal Accountant and the Supply Officer/General Services Officer Designate to refrain the processing of liquidation reports for the above-mentioned disbursements because the above disbursements may be suspended or disallowed in audit.
Management Comment:
The Management will adopt the audit recommendation as possible. The term shopping and small value procurement was used interchangeably in making the resolution as prepared by the BAC-Secretariat.

Recommendation:
We recommended to the Local Chief Executive to direct the Bids and Awards  	Committee (BAC) Secretariat to comply with the regulations and furnish the audit  	team with the  	required copy of contracts along with their supporting documents  	within the prescribed  	period for the timely review, evaluation and inspection of  	the same. Otherwise, the Audit Team will issue Notice of Suspension.
Management Comment:
The BAC Secretariat follows the rule in the submission of contract to the COA audit team for contract/technical review.

Recommendation:
We recommended to the Local Chief Executive to:
a.) 	Direct the Local Development Council to optimized the utilization of the 20%  Development Fund through proper planning of project including timing of execution; coordinated action from LGU Officials for better result; and strict    monitoring in project implementation to ensure accomplishment of the project.
b.) Ensure that expenditures should have appropriation in the Annual Budget.     Otherwise, the Audit Team will issue Notice of Disallowance.
Management Comment:
Actual loan repayments were bigger than the budgeted amount due to failure of the LBP to send schedule of loan repayments to the LGU, hence, were not included in the budget. Also, billing statements from LBP were  mostly delayed.

Recommendation:
We recommended to the Local Chief Executive to require the concerned LGU officials to prepare and submit a copy of the DILG approved annual GAD Plan and Budget (GPB) and the corresponding GAD Accomplishment Report within the period prescribed under COA Circular No. 2014-001 dated March 18, 2014.
Management Comment:
The Local Chief Executive will strictly require the concerned LGU Officials to follow the said circular in connection with the recommendation.

The Municipal Treasurer’s Office should intensify its effort to collect sufficient funds in the succeeding year to cover the cash overdraft.

b.)	The Local Chief Executive should adopt austerity measures to avoid incurrence of cash overdraft in its future operations.

c.) 	Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant to regularly remit all trust liabilities to the agencies concerned to avoid utilization of such funds for other purposes.

The Local Chief Executive should install a strong internal control system in the operations of the treasury.

He should exercise due diligence in protecting the government funds against loss or wastage.

observations.managementAction: Adopt audit recommendation

observations.recommendation: We recommended to require the Municipal Accountant to maintain special accounts in the General Fund for the operation of the Maigo Water System.
observations.managementAction: Adopt audit recommendation

observations.recommendation:

observations.managementAction: Adopt audit recommendation
observations.reason: Late submission of disbursement vouchers and supporting documents from the Municipal Treasurer’s Office.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended to require the following:

Direct the Municipal Treasurer and Accountant-Designate to  transfer the unexpended balance of LDRRMC Fund to a Special Trust Fund in the Trust Fund account solely for the purpose of supporting disaster risk reduction and management activities of the LDRRMC’s within the next five (5) years pursuant to Section  21 of RA 10121 and COA Circular No.2012-002.

Require the LDRRMC to decide and incorporate the programs, projects and activities of the unexpended LDRRMF to the Local Disaster Risk Reduction Management Fund Investment Plan (LDRRMFIP) which is prepared annually.

Instruct the Local Disaster Risk Management Officer and Municipal Accountant-Designate to submit the required reports under COA Circular N0. 2012-002, such as the LDRRMFIP (Annex A) and the Report on Utilization of DRRMF using the Annex B Format should be prepared and submitted to the agencies stated above for compliance.
observations.managementAction: Adopt audit recommendation

observations.recommendation:
Recommendation:
We recommended to direct the Municipal Treasurer to strictly comply rules and regulations in the handling and custody of cash collections, and demand immediate deposits of the remaining undeposited amount.

Recommendation:
We have recommended the following:
The Municipal Treasurer’s Office should intensify its effort to collect sufficient funds in the succeeding year to cover the cash overdraft.
b.) The Local Chief Executive should adopt austerity measures to avoid incurrence of cash overdraft in its future operations.
c.) 	Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant to regularly remit all trust liabilities to the agencies concerned to avoid utilization of such funds for other purposes.

Recommendation:
We recommended the Municipal Accountant to comply with and observe strictly to the provision of COA Circular No. 97-002. If the cash advances remained outstanding for more than a month after the completion of its purpose, demand letters should be sent out requiring the officials/employees to submit an accounting or liquidation thereof within seventy-two (72) hours. Otherwise, the whole amount should be deducted from his/her salary.

Recommendation:
We have recommended the following:
The Local Chief Executive should refrain from granting of cash advances to Elective Officials other than their traveling expenses.
Direct the Municipal Accountant to send demand letters for immediate liquidation of the outstanding balance.
Direct the Municipal Accountant to strictly require the submission of the liquidation report of the previous cash advances before another cash advances shall be granted.

Recommendation:
We have recommended the following:
Require the Municipal Treasurer to personally refund the amount of P46,800 as bank charges of NSF checks and the Audit Team will issue a corresponding Notice of Disallowance.
The Local Chief Executive should install a strong internal control system in the operations of the treasury.
He should exercise due diligence in protecting the government funds against loss or wastage.

Recommendation:
We have recommended the following:
The Local Chief Executive should create an Inventory Committee to conduct complete physical inventory count of all PPE and submit Report on the Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment (RPCPPE).
Instruct the General Services Officer/Supply Officer and the Municipal Accountant to regularly reconcile their records and review the composition of the PPE account to come up with an accurate Property, Plant and Equipment account balance.

Recommendation:
We recommended to require the General Services Officer and the Municipal Accountant to maintain Stock and Property Cards for Supplies, and Property,  Plant and Equipment Ledger Cards, respectively. The required records shall also be the basis by the GSO for its reconciliation with the actual physical inventory.

Recommendation:
We recommended that Municipal Treasurer and Collecting Officers to strictly comply the submission of the monthly and consolidated report of accountability for accountable forms.

Recommendation:
We recommended to require the Municipal Accountant to maintain special accounts in the General Fund for the operation of the Maigo Water System.

Recommendations:
We recommended to require the Municipal Accountant to strictly adhere to the prescribed time frame for the submission of monthly and quarterly reports as well as transmitting all receipts and disbursement records with complete supporting documents to the Office of the Auditor.
Also, require the Municipal Accountant to closely coordinate with the Municipal Treasurer for the latter to submit immediately the required reports within the prescribed period, and to facilitate the recording of the transactions in the books of accounts.

Recommendation:
We recommended to require the following:
Direct the Municipal Treasurer and Accountant-Designate to  transfer the unexpended balance of LDRRMC Fund to a Special Trust Fund in the Trust Fund account solely for the purpose of supporting disaster risk reduction and management activities of the LDRRMC’s within the next five (5) years pursuant to Section  21 of RA 10121 and COA Circular No.2012-002.
Require the LDRRMC to decide and incorporate the programs, projects and activities of the unexpended LDRRMF to the Local Disaster Risk Reduction Management Fund Investment Plan (LDRRMFIP) which is prepared annually.
Instruct the Local Disaster Risk Management Officer and Municipal Accountant-Designate to submit the required reports under COA Circular N0. 2012-002, such as the LDRRMFIP (Annex A) and the Report on Utilization of DRRMF using the Annex B Format should be prepared and submitted to the agencies stated above for compliance.

Recommendation:
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive and its Local School Board should strictly observed Section 272 of the Local Government Code of 1991 and DECS-DBM-DILG Joint Circular No. 01 series of 1998 on SEF utilization. It should stop providing appropriation for MOOE expenses in the Local School Board Budget which are not in accordance with the above cited law/circular, instead charge all ineligible budget items under Section 272 to the regular DepEd Budget.

Recommendation:
We recommended to require the LGU GAD Focal Point (GFP) to submit timely the GAD Accomplishment Report as illustrated under Annexes of the PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA Joint Circular No. 20013-01.
